zh.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres於1180年出生在西班牙。她和Martin Alvarez de Torres至少有1個女兒。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres于1180年出生在西班牙。她和Martin Alvarez de Torres至少有1个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Torres
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Luis, Carlos, Manuel, Jesus, Francisco, Pedro, Miguel, Jorge, Raul, Rafael.  Portuguese Ligia, Anatolio, Catarina, Paulo, Albeiro, Gonsalo, Joaquim, Lidio, Omero, Wenceslao.

Galician, Catalan, Spanish, Portuguese, and Jewish (Sephardic): habitational name from any of numerous places called Torres, all named with the plural of torre ‘tower’ (see Torre ). As a Jewish name, it was often adopted at the moment of conversion to Roman Catholicism. After the return to Judaism (generations later), some descendants retained the name their families used as Catholics. Compare De Torres .
Italian: habitational name from Torres in Belluno or Porto Torres in Sassari. In southern Italy the surname is sometimes of Spanish origin (see 1 above).

variants.name: Torrez
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Juan, Pedro, Carlos, Jesus, Armando, Raul, Ruben, Alfredo, Alvaro, Fernando.

variants.name: Torre
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Rocco, Angelo, Salvatore, Carmelo, Vito, Antonio, Carmine, Pietro, Sal, Sebastiano, Agostino, Bartolo.  Spanish Jose, Francisco, Carlos, Julio, Alvaro, Eduardo, Elvira, Ernesto, Fernando, Gregorio, Guillermo, Jorge.

Italian: topographic name for someone who lived near a tower, torre (from Latin turris), usually a defensive fortification or watchtower, or a habitational name from any of the places called with this word, as for example Torre Annunziata or Torre del Greco in Naples province, Torre de’ Passeri (Pescara, Abruzzo), or Torre di Ruggiero (Catanzaro, Calabria). Compare La Torre .

Galician, Catalan, Aragonese, Spanish, and Portuguese: topographic name from torre ‘(watch) tower’ (of the same etymology as 1), or a habitational name from any of numerous places called (La) Torre in Spain and Portugal. Compare Spanish De la Torre and La Torre .

nameMeanings.name: Dama
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Mainly North American: of unknown origin, perhaps a transferred use of a surname that is fairly common in the United States. This may be of Irish origin, although the surname is not known in Ireland. Dana or Ana was the name of an ancient Irish fertility goddess, and this was also used in medieval times as a girl's name, However, it is not clear whether there is any connection between this name and the modern given name, which is sometimes also used as a feminine form of Dan or Daniel . Modern use as a boy's given name began in honour of Richard Henry Dana ( 1815–82 ), author of Two Years before the Mast, who supported the rights of fugitive slaves before and during the Civil War. The popularity of the given name was increased by the fame of the film star Dana Andrews ( 1909–92 ).

historicEvents.description: The Christian territory controlled the Kingdoms of Aragon, Navarre, and Castile.

en.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres was born in 1180, in Spain. She had at least 1 daughter with Martin Alvarez de Torres.

fr.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres est née en 1180, à Espagne. Elle a eu au moins 1 fille avec Martin Alvarez de Torres.

de.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres wurde 1180 in Spanien geboren. Sie hatte mit Martin Alvarez de Torres mindestens 1 Tochter.

it.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres è nata il nel 1180 nel Spagna. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlia con Martin Alvarez de Torres.

ja.lifeSummary: Dama de Torresは1180年にスペインで生まれた。彼女はMartin Alvarez de Torresとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。

ru.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres родилась в 1180 г. в местности Испания. У неё была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Martin Alvarez de Torres.

es.lifeSummary: Dama de Torres nació en 1180, en España. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Martin Alvarez de Torres.
